title: Work through merge conflicts methodically
description: An ordered procedure for resolving a conflicted merge or rebase one file at a time with verification before continuing.
body: Use when git halts with conflicts during merge, rebase, or cherry-pick. Do not mark files resolved without opening them. Steps: list conflicted paths with git status; for each, inspect both sides and the base; edit to a reconciled result and remove markers; stage the file; run the build; then continue or commit. Validation signals include no remaining markers, a clean git status, and passing tests. Risk boundary: if a conflict touches security or data-handling logic, slow down and verify behavior rather than guessing, escalating when intent is unclear.
